config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan hanya karena alasan kepentingan সমাজ, karena justru hak dasar itulah yang melindungi warga dari penyalahgunaan kuasa. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat aturan yang adil, bukan dengan menomorduakan martabat dan kebebasan orang per orang. Dari pengalaman saya, ketika negara atau kelompok terlalu mudah mengatasnamakan masyarakat, yang paling sering dirugikan justru individu yang lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society's interests should automatically come first; in the United States, I value individual rights as the starting point, and I am wary of any argument that makes them easy to override. That said, I do not believe rights are absolute in every case, because public safety, public health, and basic order sometimes justify limits that are narrow and clearly defined. The problem is that "society" is often used as a vague excuse for majoritarian pressure or government overreach, which is exactly why constitutional rights matter so much. To me, the right balance is not choosing society over the individual, but forcing society to prove any restriction is necessary and fair.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu, karena di Indonesia pun saya melihat bahaya besar ketika “kepentingan masyarakat” dipakai sebagai alasan untuk menekan hak orang per orang. Memang ada keadaan tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan yang bisa membatasi hak, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sangat jelas, proporsional, dan tidak boleh jadi dalih untuk sewenang-wenang atau sekadar suara mayoritas. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari perlindungan kuat terhadap individu, terutama kelompok yang paling rentan. Kalau hak individu mudah dikalahkan atas nama kepentingan bersama, yang sering terjadi bukan harmoni, melainkan penyalahgunaan kuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the statement as written, because in the United States I see individual rights as the baseline, not something society gets to override whenever it is convenient. Society does have real interests in safety, health, and order, but those justify only narrow, evidence-based limits, not a blanket rule that collective interests come first. I also do not trust the phrase "the interests of society" very much, because it is often used to dress up majority pressure or government overreach as if it were automatically legitimate. A free society should protect individuals first and then require society to make a strong case whenever it wants to restrict those rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu, karena menurut saya di Indonesia “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai sebagai alasan untuk membungkam hak individu yang sebenarnya sah. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi dalam keadaan tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan, tetapi pembatasannya harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa diuji, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas merasa terganggu. Justru pengalaman kita menunjukkan bahwa ketika alasan kolektif diberi prioritas mutlak, yang paling mudah dikorbankan adalah kelompok kecil, lemah, atau tidak populer. Jadi, bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang selalu menomorsatukan kolektif, melainkan yang berani melindungi hak individu bahkan saat itu tidak nyaman bagi mayoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the statement as a general principle, because in the United States I do not trust "society" as a blank check to override individual rights. Public safety and public health can justify limits, but only when those limits are narrow, evidence-based, and subject to real scrutiny, not just because the majority finds a right inconvenient. What worries me most is that once collective interests are treated as automatically superior, it becomes too easy for governments or majorities to trample unpopular people and call it the common good. A healthy society does not put the individual second by default; it protects individual rights first and then proves any exception is truly necessary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
